messages.content:
Translate to Chinese (Traditional) (include romanization)

"Leech Horse"
"Leech Horse" in Traditional Chinese is "水蛭馬" (Shuǐzhì mǎ).

Translate to Chinese (Traditional)

"Coral Bits"
"Coral Bits" in Traditional Chinese is "珊瑚碎片".

Translate to Chinese (Traditional) with romanization

"Desert roadrunner"
"Desert roadrunner" in Traditional Chinese is "沙漠跑道鳥" (Shāmò pǎodào niǎo).

Translate to Chinese (Traditional)

"That's nice."
"那很好。"

Translate to Chinese (Traditional)

"It didn't get any louder."
"它沒有變得更大聲。"

Translate to Sinhala (include romanization)

"A house in the ocean."
"A house in the ocean." in Sinhala is "සමුදුරේ ගෙයක්." (Samudurē geyak.)

Translate to Sinhala (include romanization)

"Grief made him a monster"
"Grief made him a monster" in Sinhala is "දුක ඔහුට රාක්ෂයෙක් හදා ගත්තා" (Duka ohuṭa rākṣayek hadā gattā).

Translate to Odia
"He made soup"
"She made soup"
"His father made soup"
"His mother made soup"
"Her father made soup"
"Her mother made soup"

"He made soup" - "ସେ ସୁପ୍ ବନାଇଲେ" (Se sup banaile)
"She made soup" - "ସେ ସୁପ୍ ବନାଇଲେ" (Se sup banaile)
"His father made soup" - "ତାଙ୍କ ପିତା ସୁପ୍ ବନାଇଲେ" (Tanka pita sup banaile)
"His mother made soup" - "ତାଙ୍କ ମାଆ ସୁପ୍ ବନାଇଲେ" (Tanka maa sup banaile)
"Her father made soup" - "ତାଙ୍କର ପିତା ସୁପ୍ ବନାଇଲେ" (Tankara pita sup banaile)
"Her mother made soup" - "ତାଙ୍କର ମାଆ ସୁପ୍ ବନାଇଲେ" (Tankara maa sup banaile) 

Note: In Odia, "ସେ ସୁପ୍ ବନାଇଲେ" (Se sup banaile) is used for both "He made soup" and "She made soup" as gender is not explicitly indicated in this sentence structure.
Translate to Odia
Please provide the text you would like to be translated into Odia.
"Terror in the Trenches"
"Terror in the Trenches" can be translated into Odia as "ଖାଇଗଲିରେ ଆତଙ୍କ" (Khāigalire Ātaṅka).

Translate to Dutch

"In the Trenches with the Dead"
"In de loopgraven met de doden"

Translate to German

"In the Trenches with the Dead"
"In den Schützengräben mit den Toten"

Translate to Spanish

"In the Trenches with the Dead"
"En las trincheras con los muertos"

Translate to Chinese

"In the Trenches with the Dead"
"与死者同在战壕中"

Translate to Spanish

"Children of a Dead Earth"
"Hijos de una Tierra Muerta"

Translate to Spanish

"Cultures should not be confined to a nation."
"Las culturas no deberían estar confinadas a una nación."

Translate to French

"Son of the Forest"
"Fils de la forêt"

Translate to Turkish

"Son of the Forest"
"Ormanın Oğlu"

Translate to Turkish

"Fat man"
"Şişman adam"

Translate to Cantonese (include romanization)

"It was a large red man, with a beer gut."
"It was a large red man, with a beer gut." in Cantonese can be translated as "佢係一個大個頭嘅紅色男人，有啤酒肚。" (Keui hai yat go daai ge tau ge hung sik naam yan, yau be jau tou.)

Translate to Cantonese

"Orange" - as in the fruit
"Orange" - as in the colour

"Orange" as in the fruit - "橙" (chaang)
"Orange" as in the colour - "橙色" (chaang sik)

Translate to Cantonese
"Blessed by the gods"
"Blessed by the gods" in Cantonese can be translated as "得到神嘅祝福" (dak dou san ge juk fuk).

Translate to Chinese (Taiwanese)

"Seasonal Depression"
"Seasonal Depression" in Mandarin as used in Taiwan can be translated as "季節性憂鬱" (Jìjiéxìng yōuyù).

Translate to Chinese (Taiwanese)

"There, now it's art!"
"There, now it's art!" in Mandarin as used in Taiwan would be "看，現在它成為藝術了！" (Kàn, xiànzài tā chéngwéi yìshù le!)

Translate to Chinese (Taiwanese)

"NIGHT TRAIN"
"NIGHT TRAIN" in Taiwanese Mandarin can be translated as "夜間列車" (Yèjiān lièchē).

Translate to Chinese (Taiwanese)

"Crocodile"
"Crocodile" in Taiwanese Mandarin is "鱷魚" (Èyú).

Translate to Turkish

"Information Program"
"Bilgi Programı"
Translate to
It seems like you haven't provided any text for translation. Could you please provide the text you want to be translated?
It looks like you might have accidentally submitted an empty message. How can I assist you today?

Translate to Chinese (Taiwan)

"The broadcast will begin shortly, thank you for your patience."
"廣播將於稍後開始，感謝您的耐心等待。"
